channel.title: The Grant Desk - next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: next 30 days.

item.title: A.R.M. Holding / Art Dubai Projects: H Residence Sculpture Park Commission (Dubai)
item.description:
Organisation: A.R.M. Holding / HUNA / Art Dubai Projects

Location: H Residence Sculpture Park, Dubai, UAE. Open to UAE-based, regional and international artists.

Award: AED 1,000,000 commission envelope, inclusive of artist fee, design development, fabrication, materials, production, transport, installation, lighting and finishing. Proposal fees are available for shortlisted artists. No submission fee.

Duration: Proposal development September 2026; fabrication autumn 2026; unveiling 2 December 2026

Deadline: 25 Jul 2026

A.R.M. Holding and HUNA, with the commission supported and managed by Art Dubai Projects, invite artists to propose a permanent public artwork for the H Residence Sculpture Park in Dubai, the anchor piece of the park's programme. Artists respond to the theme 'We Emerge Stronger than Before' through a permanent, site-specific outdoor sculpture; the strongest proposals will find what the phrase opens up (continuity, endurance, renewal, collective presence, the long view) through material, form, scale and the conditions of the site. Where language or text is incorporated, Arabic should lead. TECHNICAL: footprint minimum around 15 sqm, up to 3 m high; weight restriction 5.5 kN/m2; must be suitable for long-term outdoor display in Dubai (summer over 40 C, humidity to 90 percent, about six hours direct sun daily); presence by day and night. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists with or without gallery representation; UAE-based, regional and international artists welcome; prior experience with permanent or large-scale outdoor/public installation required. BUDGET: AED 1,000,000 all-inclusive; shortlisted artists receive full budget parameters and proposal fees at the proposal development stage. SUBMISSION: CV and portfolio (attention to outdoor, large-scale or permanent commissions), concept statement (max 300 words), a render or visualisation in context plus at least one sketch, technical specifications, and a site response. TIMELINE: submissions close 25 July 2026; shortlist notified from 10 August; commission awarded after September proposal development; work unveiled at H Residence on 2 December 2026. Local fabrication strongly preferred. Questions: Shahad@artdubai.ae.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: British Council Ireland: UK-IRL Culture Connects Grant 2026/27
item.description:
Organisation: British Council Ireland / Arts Council Ireland

Location: Ireland and the UK. For bilateral partnerships of one individual based in Ireland and one based in England, Northern Ireland, Scotland or Wales; in-person collaboration required.

Award: Up to EUR 3,000 per collaboration (smaller amounts also eligible), covering artist fees (including your own time), collaborator fees, travel and accommodation, studio or space hire, materials, and access costs including disability support. No application fee.

Duration: Activity between October 2026 and March 2027; must be completed by 31 March 2027

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The UK-IRL Culture Connects Grant 2026/27 is a British Council Ireland initiative delivered in association with Arts Council Ireland, supporting the early stages of artistic collaboration between Ireland and the four UK nations through in-person exchange. It prioritises creative development over final outputs: research and development of new ideas, mentorship, exploring collaborative pathways with producers or partners, experimenting with new technologies or practices, and developing initiatives that support creative exchange. WHO: applications are made by individual artists or creative practitioners working as a bilateral partnership of one individual based in Ireland and one based in England, Northern Ireland, Scotland or Wales; applicants must be resident in Ireland or the UK for the project duration, have a proven professional track record, be at an early or developing career stage, and not be in full-time education. One application per collaboration. FUNDING: up to EUR 3,000 covering artist and collaborator fees, travel and accommodation, studio hire, materials, and access costs. Activities must involve in-person collaboration (fully remote projects are not eligible) and take place between October 2026 and 31 March 2027. Apply via the British Council GoodGrants platform; an information webinar recording and overview document are on the programme page. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Modern Art Oxford: Collaborative Micro-commissions Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Modern Art Oxford

Location: Oxford, UK. Open to collectives, duos, partnerships and collaborative groups based in the Arts Council England South East area (Oxfordshire, Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Hampshire and Isle of Wight, Surrey, Kent, East Sussex, West Sussex).

Award: GBP 1,000 artist fee per selected collective, plus up to GBP 500 production budget, curatorial mentorship from the Modern Art Oxford team, four months of public exhibition display, professional installation photography, promotion, technical and production support, artwork transport support within the South East, peer networking, and inclusion in a public preview event. No application fee.

Duration: Production August to November 2026; install 23 November to 4 December 2026; exhibition 5 December 2026 to April 2027

Deadline: 26 Jul 2026

Modern Art Oxford marks its 60th anniversary with an open call for three collaborative micro-commissions responding to the question: how can collective practice/s help us to embrace uncertainty and complexity? Collectives, duos, partnerships and collaborative groups based across the South East of England are invited to propose new small-scale experimental works for a group exhibition in the Ground Floor Gallery, running alongside a major solo exhibition by Mike Nelson, who is also on the selection panel. Proposals may take the form of multidisciplinary research, site-responsive work, grassroots organising, performance, visual art, sound, poetry or other creative practices; ideas should respond to the theme of collective practice and uncertainty, explore collaborative approaches, experiment across disciplines, be achievable within the budget and timeline, and be original to this open call (building on existing research is fine). WHO: groups of two or more people, including established artist collectives, newly formed collaborations, interdisciplinary partnerships, informal or community-led groups, and family collaborations, based in the Arts Council England South East area (Oxfordshire, Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Hampshire and Isle of Wight, Surrey, Kent, East Sussex, West Sussex). SUPPORT: each of the three selected collectives receives a GBP 1,000 artist fee, up to GBP 500 production budget, curatorial mentorship, four months of exhibition display, installation photography, promotion, technical and production support, and transport support within the South East. TIMELINE: online briefing and Q&A 15 June 2026 (email creativelearning@modernartoxford.org.uk for the link); selection confirmed 14 August 2026; production August to November 2026; preview party 4 December 2026; exhibition 5 December 2026 to April 2027. Apply via Google Forms; an information pack PDF and FAQ are on the call page. DEADLINE: Sunday 26 July 2026, 23:59.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AVISTAVULCÃO: Azores Artist Residency 2027 (Faial, Portugal)
item.description:
Organisation: AVISTAVULCÃO / Barca 13

Location: Faial Island, Azores, Portugal (near the Capelinhos Volcano). Open to artists from countries eligible for the Creative Europe programme.

Award: EUR 1,350 allowance per resident for the month, plus travel allowance (EUR 400 to 800), green mobility top-up (EUR 400), visa top-up (EUR 120/resident), outermost regions and overseas territories top-up (EUR 175/resident), family top-up (EUR 200 per child/resident), and accessibility support. No application fee.

Duration: One month, March 2027

Deadline: 01 Aug 2026

AVISTAVULCÃO opens a call for a one-month artist-in-residence programme focused on research and creation, taking place in March 2027 and intended for an artist or artistic duo. Located on the island of Faial near the Capelinhos Volcano (Azores, Portugal), the residency offers immersion in a territory shaped by geology, insularity, and the interplay between nature and human presence, with the Azores treated as living material for creation. Artists are invited to develop projects engaging with landscape, memory, local history, and the contemporary challenges facing peripheral regions; suggested starting points include geological time, transformation, resilience, isolation, mobility, and the relationships between communities and the environment. Context-sensitive proposals that prioritise observation, listening and a relationship with the place are valued. Residents meet local stakeholders and share moments with the community, culminating in a public presentation of the process or results. SUPPORT (in-person mobility): EUR 1,350 allowance per resident, travel allowance of EUR 400 to 800, green mobility top-up of EUR 400, visa top-up of EUR 120 per resident, outermost regions top-up of EUR 175 per resident, family top-up of EUR 200 per child, and accessibility support. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and duos (cross-disciplinary welcome) from countries eligible for the Creative Europe programme. DEADLINE: 1 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Black Arts Centre: Call for Exhibition and Programming Proposals
item.description:
Organisation: Black Arts Centre

Location: Surrey, British Columbia, Canada. Priority to Black artists, curators, and art organizers, and to projects aligned with the gallery's mandate (BIPOC, Disabled, Queer, Immigrant/Refugee/Newcomer perspectives).

Award: Successful proposals receive financial, budgeting, and curatorial support from the Black Arts Centre, including project execution, production, logistics, and marketing. No submission fee.

Duration: Exhibition proposals considered for Spring 2027 and beyond; programming proposals for projects taking place from September 2026 onward. Submission portal open 1 June to 8 August 2026; applicants begin to hear back in September.

Deadline: 08 Aug 2026

The Black Arts Centre is calling for proposals from artists for exhibitions and public programming. Exhibition proposals are considered for Spring 2027 and beyond, while programming proposals are considered for projects taking place from September 2026 onward. The submission portal is open from 1 June to 8 August 2026. Proposals are reviewed by the Black Arts Centre team and a revolving committee of external community members, evaluated using an internal rubric; applicants begin to hear back in September. SUPPORT: successful proposals receive financial, budgeting, and curatorial support from the Black Arts Centre, including project execution, production, logistics, and marketing. ELIGIBILITY AND PRIORITY: applicants do not need to identify as Black to submit, but priority is given to Black artists, curators, and art organizers, and to artists and projects that align with the gallery's mandate (BIPOC, Disabled, Queer, Immigrant/Refugee/Newcomer perspectives). Submission does not guarantee approval for this cycle, but all proposals are kept on file for future consideration, and updated or alternate proposals are accepted. Questions and PDF proposals/portfolios: info@theblackartscentre.ca. Deadline 8 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Railyard Art Project: Call for Temporary Public Art (Santa Fe)
item.description:
Organisation: Railyard Art Project (Railyard Park + Plaza)

Location: Santa Fe, New Mexico, United States (Railyard Park + Plaza, City-owned property under a Conservation Easement).

Award: USD 3,500 honorarium per project (two projects selected), plus marketing support and liability insurance. Funded by Santa Fe's Arts and Culture Commission. No application fee.

Duration: Two projects hosted between October 2026 and June 2027.

Deadline: 05 Aug 2026

The Railyard Art Project (RAP) facilitates temporary public art installations by established and emerging artists and arts collectives in Santa Fe's Railyard Park + Plaza. RAP is accepting proposals for temporary public art installations and creative projects; thanks to funding from Santa Fe's Arts and Culture Commission, two projects will be hosted between October 2026 and June 2027, each receiving a USD 3,500 honorarium plus marketing support and liability insurance. THEMES: the RAP Committee encourages collaboration across disciplines (art, science, history) and welcomes thoughtful, challenging, playful, and edifying art in the context of social and cultural expression and environmental sustainability. Proposed projects must physically withstand outdoor public display and engage the community by responding to the natural and social landscape of the park. SELECTION CRITERIA: overall artistic merit; context and familiarity with the proposed site; practical viability; and potential for community engagement and cultural/educational enrichment. Artists should select an eligible area of the park's open space and describe the project in detail; photos or renderings may be uploaded with the application (a map of Railyard Art Locations is linked from the program page). The RAP Committee, made up of community members and Park + Plaza stakeholders, selects and schedules the exhibitions; a formal in-person presentation may be requested depending on complexity. CONDITIONS: the park operates under a Conservation Easement, so installations cannot be located on planted areas, no staking of any kind is permitted (including during install/de-install), and nothing may be hung from trees. Artists lend artwork at their own risk (no protection against vandalism or weather damage, and artists are responsible for repairs). Installs and de-installs must be coordinated in advance with the RAP Committee or Railyard Park Conservancy staff, with a Committee or staff member on site throughout. Application deadline 5 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Target Margin Theater: TMT Institute Fellowship 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Target Margin Theater

Location: Brooklyn, New York, United States (Sunset Park). Fellows must be available in person for intensives, monthly breakfasts, and excursions.

Award: USD 2,000 stipend, plus space, material resources, and collaborators to support each Fellow's experiments. No application fee.

Duration: 2026-27 season: three intensive sessions in Brooklyn (5-8 November 2026, 8-10 January 2027, 19-21 March 2027), monthly breakfasts October 2026 through May 2027, and an Open Studio weekend in June 2027.

Deadline: 22 Jul 2026

Target Margin Theater invites applications for its next cohort of TMT Institute Fellows for the 2026/2027 season. The Institute is a cohort of artists who seek to radically disrupt and re-examine their existing practice without any goal or expected result: pure experimentation with no product-oriented end goal. Fellows pursue no project, no developmental stage, no deliverable; misfires, dead ends, and bad ideas are considered a fruitful part of the work. SUPPORT: TMT provides space, material resources, collaborators, and a USD 2,000 stipend. STRUCTURE: three short intensive sessions at Target Margin Theater in Brooklyn (5-8 November 2026, 8-10 January 2027, and 19-21 March 2027, all day with some evening hours), monthly breakfasts at Junior's in Brooklyn (20 October, 30 November, 4 January, 1 February, 8 March, 5 April, 3 May), and occasional excursions. After the second intensive, each Fellow commits to an exploration pursued outside group gatherings, supported by TMT resources. The season ends with an Open Studio weekend in June 2027 to share explorations with the community; this is not a performance and should not be an audience-attended, time-bounded show. WHO CAN APPLY: artists from any discipline within or outside theater, as well as production, technical, and administrative workers in the arts. Applicants must have an established, existing body of work and practice they are committed to changing; do not apply with a project. Recent graduates of training programs are unlikely to be appropriate candidates; no formal education level or degree is required. SELECTION: TMT staff and artistic community review applications and invite a handful of candidates to interview; all applicants are notified by October. Deadline: Wednesday 22 July 2026, 11:59 pm EST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Arts Council of Ireland: Visual Arts Project Award 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Ireland / An Chomhairle Ealaion

Location: Republic of Ireland. Applicants based outside Ireland may apply if the application clearly shows how outcomes benefit the arts in the Republic of Ireland.

Award: Three strands: up to EUR 12,000 (planning and pre-production), up to EUR 60,000 (production and engagement), up to EUR 150,000 (partnership production). No application fee.

Duration: Projects delivered in 2027.

Deadline: 23 Jul 2026

The Arts Council of Ireland's Visual Arts Project Award funds professional visual artists, curators, and producers to develop and deliver visual arts projects, in three strands: planning and pre-production (up to EUR 12,000), production and engagement (up to EUR 60,000), and partnership production (up to EUR 150,000). Primarily aimed at applicants based in the Republic of Ireland, but the Arts Council's standard eligibility clause allows applicants based elsewhere where the application explicitly demonstrates how the outcomes will benefit the arts in Ireland. The wider Project Award 2027 covers nine disciplines including film and music on a similar timeline. Deadline 23 July 2026, 17:30 Irish time.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Global Artivism: Governance Fellow
item.description:
Organisation: Global Artivism (a fiscally sponsored project of Res Publica US Inc.)

Location: Remote, open globally. Priority given to applicants from the Global South.

Award: USD 1,200 to 1,800 per month (independent contractor fee), paid on receipt of monthly invoices. No employee benefits, paid leave, or insurance; contractor covers their own taxes and compliance.

Duration: 12-month independent contractor engagement from August 2026, with potential renewal subject to funding and mutual agreement. Flexible hours with scheduled calls across time zones.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Global Artivism, a Global Majority-led movement that engages the power of arts and culture toward a just world on a safe planet, is contracting a Governance Fellow for a mentored, time-limited engagement focused on governance systems, democratic institutional design, and Global Majority-led movement infrastructure. The contractor supports the development and maintenance of Global Artivism's governance architecture, including documentation, committee coordination, and research that supports the Democratic Transition process, and is expected to take initiative on assigned tasks and grow through structured mentorship. The movement is governed by a Global Council with a Movement Secretariat coordination team; the Fellow coordinates primarily with the Governance Lead alongside the Operations Director, Secretariat members, and Council committees (coordination relationships do not constitute supervisory authority). KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: governance documentation (frameworks, committee charters, terms of reference, process documentation; maintaining an organized, current, consistently formatted document library; preparing materials for Global Council meetings); committee coordination (scheduling, materials, meeting notes, follow-up, trackers of decisions and action items, agendas and briefing packs); Democratic Transition and electoral process support (research on governance models, electoral processes, and democratic participation mechanisms; drafting materials for the Democratic Transition Roadmap 2025-2027; preparing electoral framework and testing documentation; tracking milestones); and governance research (comparative analyses and briefing notes; staying current on distributed governance, participatory democracy, and decentralized leadership; contributing to knowledge products). REQUIRED: manages multiple documentation tasks and meets deadlines with attention to accuracy and formatting (experience via union organizing, participatory facilitation, cooperative governance, or community-based decision-making counts alongside academic or formal work history); good written English; ability to take initiative and work independently; genuine interest in governance, movement building, or institutional design; ability to operate as an independent contractor; and commitment to Global Artivism's values. PREFERRED: background in governance, law, political science, or international development; interest in Global Majority leadership and distributed power; civil society/NGO/movement experience; familiarity with arts, culture, or social justice movements; multilingual ability in languages relevant to Global South contexts. This is an independent contractor engagement managed through Global Artivism's fiscal sponsorship with Res Publica US Inc.; the contractor sets their own hours and methods, may serve other clients, and handles their own taxes and local compliance. TO APPLY: submit an expression of interest via the ABM Intelligence application page; submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis by ABM's consulting team. Expressions of interest close Thursday 16 July 2026, 10pm MYT (UTC+8); late submissions will not be reviewed. Priority is given to candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities, and community organizing, grassroots coordination, and self-taught practice are valued alongside formal credentials.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Global Artivism: Operations Fellow
item.description:
Organisation: Global Artivism (a fiscally sponsored project of Res Publica US Inc.)

Location: Remote, open globally. Priority given to applicants from the Global South.

Award: USD 1,000 to 1,500 per month (independent contractor fee), paid on receipt of monthly invoices. No employee benefits, paid leave, or insurance; contractor covers their own taxes and compliance.

Duration: 12-month independent contractor engagement from August 2026, with potential renewal subject to funding and mutual agreement. Flexible hours with scheduled calls across time zones.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Global Artivism, a Global Majority-led movement that engages the power of arts and culture toward a just world on a safe planet, is contracting an Operations Fellow for a mentored, time-limited engagement focused on organizational infrastructure and systems design. Working alongside the Operations Director, the contractor designs operational frameworks spanning financial workflows, contractor management, governance alignment, and onboarding, contributing directly to infrastructure decisions while building practical experience in how a Global Majority-led movement organization functions operationally. The Fellow coordinates primarily with the Operations Director alongside the Governance Lead, Project Manager, Admin Coordinator, and Secretariat members (coordination relationships do not constitute supervisory authority; decision-making is distributed through governance structures and committees). KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: infrastructure design and framework development (designing and documenting operational frameworks; contributing to staffing models, organizational structures, and resource frameworks that enable both centralized and decentralized work; designing onboarding and operational guidance; aligning operational systems with governance frameworks and democratic decision-making); systems and knowledge documentation (operational guidance and toolkits for distributed teams; internal knowledge systems such as documentation, checklists, and decision trees); and capacity building and research (identifying where documentation or systems redesign enables teams to work more independently; flagging operational bottlenecks and proposing solutions; sharing learning with Council, committees, and teams; researching operational models relevant to Global Artivism's structure). REQUIRED: manages multiple tasks and meets deadlines with attention to detail and comfort coordinating logistics across a distributed team (experience via coordinating community events, grassroots logistics, or operations in any context counts alongside formal work history); good written English; comfort with digital tools (Google Workspace, Slack, or similar); genuine interest in operations, organizational systems, or movement infrastructure; ability to operate as an independent contractor; and commitment to Global Artivism's values. PREFERRED: background in business administration, project management, or finance; interest in international civil society, movement building, or Global Majority leadership; civil society/NGO/community experience; familiarity with digital project management or operations tools; multilingual ability in languages relevant to Global South contexts. This is an independent contractor engagement managed through Global Artivism's fiscal sponsorship with Res Publica US Inc.; the contractor sets their own hours and methods, may serve other clients, and handles their own taxes and local compliance. TO APPLY: submit an expression of interest via the ABM Intelligence application page; submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis by ABM's consulting team. Expressions of interest close Thursday 16 July 2026, 10pm MYT (UTC+8); late submissions will not be reviewed. Priority is given to candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities, and community organizing, grassroots coordination, and self-taught practice are valued alongside formal credentials.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Publication
item.description:
Organisation: Mondriaan Fund (Mondriaan Fonds)

Location: Netherlands. Featured artist(s) must be professional visual artists based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom; publishers may co-apply.

Award: Contribution of up to 35% of eligible expenses. Total budget for this round is EUR 205,000.

Duration: Project-based (production of a publication).

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

The Mondriaan Fund's Publication grant supports the production of special publications about living visual artists from the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. It funds books and other publications that give substantial, high-quality attention to an artist's work and practice. WHO CAN APPLY: visual artists and publishers, individually or jointly; the featured artist(s) must be professional and have been active for at least one year, based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. AMOUNT: the Mondriaan Fund contributes a maximum of 35% of the eligible expenses; the available budget for this round is EUR 205,000. APPLYING: submitted through the Mondriaan Fund's online application portal, which requires a pre-created account (allow several working days to set up). DEADLINE: 30 July 2026, 16:00 Dutch time (10:00 Caribbean time).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ucross General Residency (All Disciplines): Spring 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Ucross Foundation

Location: Sheridan, Wyoming, USA

Award: No charge for the residency; living accommodations, meals, and work space provided. USD 40 application fee.

Duration: Spring session (February through early June 2027); residencies range from two to six weeks

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Ucross offers fully funded artist residencies on a working cattle ranch in the foothills of Wyoming's Bighorn Mountains, providing uninterrupted time and space free from the pressures and distractions of daily life. Founded in 1981 with its first residency period in 1983, Ucross hosts approximately 115 individuals per year. Residencies range from two to six weeks; at any one time up to ten people are in residence, typically four writers, four visual artists, and two composers. Each artist receives living accommodations, meals, and work space at no charge. Lunches and dinners are prepared Monday to Friday by a professional chef (lunches delivered to studios, group dinners at 6 p.m.), with ample provisions for breakfasts and weekends; towels, linens, and weekly housekeeping are provided, and there is cell service and wireless internet throughout. Residents provide their own working materials and arrange their own travel to Sheridan, Wyoming. The program is open to visual artists, writers, composers, choreographers, interdisciplinary artists, performance artists, and collaborative teams; applicants must exhibit professional standing in their field, and both established and emerging artists are encouraged to apply. Applications are submitted by discipline category: dance, music/sound, performance, film/video, playwriting/screenwriting, visual art, fiction, nonfiction, poetry, or interdisciplinary. The work sample and project proposal are the most significant features of the application and should correspond to one another; if your work is in a single discipline, select the category that best fits your project, and apply under interdisciplinary only if the project genuinely combines multiple disciplines. Work-sample requirements by category: DANCE, 2-3 video files (each up to 8 minutes and 200 MB, or up to 3 Vimeo links, with a timestamp if longer than 8 minutes); MUSIC/SOUND, 3-5 audio files (each up to 8 minutes and 200 MB, or up to 3 Vimeo links; musical scores welcome); PERFORMANCE and FILM/VIDEO, 1-3 video files (each up to 5 minutes and under 200 MB, or up to 3 Vimeo/YouTube links, with a timestamp if longer than 5 minutes); PLAYWRITING/SCREENWRITING, one complete play double-spaced noting the 20-page section to read; VISUAL ART, 10 images at 4x6 inches and 300 dpi (JPG or TIF, no PDFs) plus a data sheet listing title, medium, size, and year; FICTION and NONFICTION, 20 pages of double-spaced writing; POETRY, 6-10 poems (10 maximum) in one PDF, each poem starting on a new page, total not exceeding 15 pages; INTERDISCIPLINARY, samples representing the genres you plan to work in, combining any of the above. All written work samples must be in English. There is a USD 40 application fee. The open call for the Spring 2027 session is open May 1 to July 15, 2026 (apply by 11:59 p.m. MT on July 15). Dedicated Fellowships for Native American Visual Artists and Writers and a separate Culinary Residency are also offered.

Open call details

item.title: Ucross Fellowship for Native American Writers: Spring 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Ucross Foundation

Location: Sheridan, Wyoming, USA

Award: Fully funded fellowship residency; living accommodations, meals, and work space provided. No application fee.

Duration: Spring session (February through early June 2027); residencies range from two to six weeks

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

The Ucross Fellowship for Native American Writers is a dedicated fellowship within Ucross Foundation's residency program, offering fully funded time and space to write on a working cattle ranch in the foothills of Wyoming's Bighorn Mountains. The fellowship provides living accommodations, meals, and work space at no charge, with no application fee. One Fellowship winner is selected per session; all applicants also have the option of being considered for a general Ucross residency. ELIGIBILITY: open to Native American writers who are practicing contemporary writers currently producing work in one or more genres, including but not limited to fiction, nonfiction, poetry, drama, screenwriting, playwriting, and hybrid forms; and who are enrolled members of a state-recognized or federally-recognized Tribe, Pueblo, Nation, Native Community, Political Entity, or Alaskan Native Village. The work sample and project description are the most significant features of the application. Unless the work is genuinely interdisciplinary (the genres interconnect), applicants are encouraged to apply in a single primary discipline and submit a work sample and project description emphasizing that discipline. Work-sample requirements: FICTION, 20 pages of double-spaced fiction (a novel excerpt, a story, several stories, or a combination); NONFICTION, 20 pages of double-spaced nonfiction; POETRY, 10 pages of poetry (may be single-spaced); PLAYWRITING, one complete play (production documentation may be included if relevant), noting the 20 pages for reviewers to read; SCREENWRITING, one complete screenplay (production documentation may be included if relevant), noting the 20 pages for reviewers to read. All writing samples should include your full name. The open call for the Spring 2027 session closes July 15, 2026 (apply by 11:59 p.m. MT). A separate Ucross Fellowship for Native American Visual Artists is also offered.

Open call details

item.title: Akademie Schloss Solitude: Web Residencies No. 22 - Ignore All Previous Instructions
item.description:
Organisation: Akademie Schloss Solitude

Location: Online (fully remote). Awarded by Akademie Schloss Solitude, Stuttgart, Germany.

Award: EUR 1,200 grant for a four-week online residency, plus a microsite to develop and publish the project, and support from a web/UX designer and a programmer in a supportive capacity.

Duration: Four-week online residency.

Deadline: 07 Aug 2026

Web Residencies No. 22, 'Ignore All Previous Instructions,' is curated by !Mediengruppe Bitnik as part of Akademie Schloss Solitude's Web Residencies program, which since 2016 has supported young talents from the international digital scene as well as artists of all disciplines engaging with web-based practices. For the tenth anniversary of the program, this edition invites artists to create works that challenge extractive digital platforms and AI systems, exploring how to interrupt dominant technological logics, from ad-tech surveillance to prompt injection vulnerabilities, and imagine alternative structures and communication methods. The open call seeks proposals that hijack, glitch, and disrupt rather than comply with existing systems, drawing on strategies like Situationist detournement to map routes through and around the grids we are trapped within. Four to six proposals will be selected. BENEFITS: a four-week online residency; a grant of EUR 1,200; a web space (microsite) on which the project can be developed and published online; support from a web/UX designer and a programmer (in a supportive capacity only, not to build an entire website); and participation in individual and group online events with the curators and Solitude fellows. Offline or hybrid formats (a reading session, panel, exhibition, or curated program) may follow. ELIGIBILITY: artists of all disciplines may apply, with no age limit; submission is free of charge. The residency takes place exclusively online, and selected fellows agree to be available online during the four weeks. TIMELINE: the open call runs from July 7 to August 7, 2026.

Open call details

item.description:

Location: Villorba (Treviso), Italy.

Award: Fully funded residency covering mentoring, accommodation, travel costs, production costs, and a daily salary. No application fee.

Duration: One-month residency, 1-31 March 2027.

Deadline: 19 Jul 2026

Villa Filanda Antonini launches its Spring Open Call to select up to four artists and professionals for a one-month residency in March 2027, focused on the theme "Sonic Connections." Hosted at Villa Filanda Antonini, a former silk mill in Villorba, Italy, the residency centres on experimental music, sound art, and performance, bringing together four international artists and professionals to examine the relationship between architecture, landscape, and collective sonic experience. ADDRESSED TO: sound artists, performers, curators, professionals, and designers who have completed their studies and reside in Europe (excluding Italy) and in OCTs/ORs countries. GRANT & BENEFITS: fully funded residency covering mentoring, accommodation, travel costs, production costs, and a daily salary. WHEN: 1 March to 31 March 2027. HOW TO APPLY: complete the application form and attach your CV, portfolio, project proposal, and a letter of reference. DEADLINE: 19 July 2026, 23:59 CEST.

item.title: Paid Research Assistant: AI and the War in Ukraine
item.description:
Organisation: Bogna Konior (NYU Shanghai)

Location: Remote-friendly.

Award: USD 18 per hour; 10-15 hours per week, flexible schedule.

Duration: Ongoing part-time research assistantship; start as soon as possible.

Deadline: 23 Jul 2026

Bogna Konior, Assistant Professor of Media Theory at NYU Shanghai, is hiring a paid research assistant for a project on Ukraine and artificial intelligence. The work supports a forthcoming book with MIT Press, plus an independent scenario she is writing on the future of AI with the war as a lens. WHAT THE WORK LOOKS LIKE: going month-by-month through news archives (New York Times, Reuters, Financial Times, and specialist outlets), annotating and cataloguing coverage of AI, drones, and autonomous systems in the war, plus other developments she will direct you to; building and maintaining a structured Notion database covering chronology, companies, industries, and key actors; and organizing and editing her existing notes. WHO SHE IS LOOKING FOR: interest in and general knowledge of AI; prior research and annotation experience, careful and detail-oriented, no sloppy citations; comfortable with Notion and with AI tools; Ukrainian language skill a strong plus (not required); familiarity with AI, war, tech, and geopolitics news sources is great. Remote-friendly, start ASAP. She will also consider people with no prior experience, as well as current and previous students. TERMS: USD 18 per hour, 10-15 hours per week, flexible schedule. TO APPLY: email bogna@nyu.edu with a short introduction and background (link to website, socials, CV, LinkedIn, or portfolio if you have one); where you are based and your weekly availability; and one recent AI-related story, company, or development you find interesting and why (a vibe check). Bonus: a sample of past research or writing if you have it.

Open call details

item.title: Alabama Historical Commission: Clotilda Memorial Public Art Commission
item.description:
Organisation: Alabama Historical Commission (with Forecast Public Art)

Location: Africatown, Mobile, Alabama, USA; open to artists internationally.

Award: USD 2,000,000 total project budget for a paid public art commission. No application fee.

Duration: Permanent public art commission

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

The Alabama Historical Commission, working with public-art consultancy Forecast Public Art, seeks proposals for a major memorial artwork honoring the survivors of the Clotilda, the last known ship to illegally traffic enslaved Africans to the United States (1860). The memorial will convey the survivors' lives and legacies and be developed through collaborative community engagement with historic Africatown, the community founded by Clotilda survivors, and the broader diaspora. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists of all disciplines, including time-based and media artists; individuals and teams; open internationally. BUDGET: USD 2,000,000 total project budget. DEADLINE: 9 August 2026, 11:59pm CST. Applications are handled through the CODAworx RFQ platform; find the Clotilda Memorial listing in the CODAworx opportunities index. Verify the listing details on CODAworx before applying.

item.title: AGI Governance Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Johns Hopkins University, School of Government and Policy (SOGP)

Location: Hopkins Bloomberg Center, Washington, D.C. (in-person only)

Award: Fully funded: travel, accommodation, and meals covered.

Duration: Three-week in-person intensive, 8-25 September 2026

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

The AGI Governance Fellowship is the inaugural three-week intensive run by Johns Hopkins University's School of Government and Policy (SOGP) to develop the next generation of leaders in AI governance. COVERS: frontier AI legislation at state and federal levels; building societal resilience amid rapid capability growth; democratic practices in AGI alignment; and designing new governing institutions for the AGI era. WHO SHOULD APPLY: early-career researchers and practitioners (within roughly eight years of undergrad) with solid foundations in AI governance, including congressional staffers, AGI company employees, recent CS or policy PhDs, and nonprofit/civil society professionals; a postgraduate degree or equivalent experience is required. This is a capstone program for rising leaders, not an intro course. FUNDING: travel, accommodation, and meals are fully covered. DATES: 8-25 September 2026 at the Hopkins Bloomberg Center in Washington, D.C. (in-person only). DEADLINE: apply by 15 July 2026 for full consideration; rolling applications accepted. APPLY: https://sogp.jh.edu/agi-governance-fellowship/

Open call details

item.title: Mountainfilm: Commitment Grants & Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Mountainfilm

Location: Application online; Telluride, Colorado, United States (fellowship trip during the festival). Commitment Grants are open to filmmakers from any country; the Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship requires current U.S. residency.

Award: Over USD 90,000 in total funding. Individual Commitment Grants range from USD 3,000 to 6,000. The Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship adds a yearlong mentorship, a cash award, and a funded trip to Telluride over Memorial Day weekend (travel, lodging, and a festival pass).

Duration: Commitment Grants support projects in post-production; the Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship is a yearlong mentorship.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Mountainfilm's Commitment Grants support documentary filmmakers with projects in post-production, with over USD 90,000 in funding this cycle for films that celebrate adventure, activism, social justice, culture, the environment, and indomitable spirit. Mountainfilm actively encourages diversity behind the camera, and a portion of funding is set aside for two focus areas: projects featuring an LGBTQ+ protagonist or a story about overcoming personal challenges, societal stigmas, and/or community obstacles with a meaningful connection to the outdoors or nature-based pursuits; and projects exploring immigration or migration, or created by immigrant or first-generation immigrant filmmakers. AWARDS: individual Commitment Grants range from USD 3,000 to 6,000. EMERGING FILMMAKER FELLOWSHIP: one early-career filmmaker is selected from the Commitment Grant applicant pool for a yearlong mentorship that includes edit and story consultation, networking opportunities, a cash award, and a funded trip to Telluride during the festival over Memorial Day weekend (travel, lodging, and a Mountainfilm pass, applicable to the year following the award). ELIGIBILITY: Commitment Grants are open to applicants from any country; the Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship accepts applicants from any country but requires current U.S. residency. Post-production projects only, completed, released, or festival-screened projects are not eligible, and impact campaigns are not funded. Prior Commitment Grant recipients should not re-apply for the same project; previously declined projects may re-apply only with substantial changes. REQUIREMENTS: a video sample or trailer of the current project no longer than 10 minutes (password-protected Vimeo links preferred); the screener link must stay active and cannot be changed after submission. The Emerging Filmmaker application additionally requires a director bio, headshot, and cover letter. Recipients are expected to provide progress updates 2-3 times a year, include Mountainfilm recognition in credits, and discuss premiere consideration with the programming team. TIMELINE: applications are open July 1 to July 16, 2026 (closing 11:59 PM MDT), with notifications in late August or early September. Questions: entries@mountainfilm.org.

Open call details

item.title: Horizon Institute for Public Service: AI Rapid Response Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Horizon Institute for Public Service

Location: Washington, DC area, United States (in-person; full-time placements in U.S. executive branch offices).

Award: Fellow track: USD 170,000 to 200,000+, plus an additional stipend for benefits. Senior Fellow track: from USD 250,000+, plus a benefits stipend, commensurate with the seniority of the placement.

Duration: One year full-time, with the opportunity for renewal.

Deadline: 22 Jul 2026

The AI Rapid Response Fellowship is a focused effort by the Horizon Institute for Public Service to close the AI security talent gap in the U.S. federal government. Horizon is recruiting a specialized cohort of exceptional technical and policy talent to work full-time in executive branch offices on AI security-related issues, with placements beginning as soon as possible. TRACKS: the Fellow track is for candidates with technical or policy expertise relevant to AI security, typically 2-6+ years of full-time relevant experience; the Senior Fellow track is for candidates with substantial prior experience, typically 10-15+ years in relevant technical, policy, or leadership roles. FOCUS: AI governance, AI security, policy, and public service. COMPENSATION: Fellow track USD 170,000-200,000+ plus a benefits stipend; Senior Fellow track from USD 250,000+ plus a benefits stipend, commensurate with the seniority of the placement. DURATION: one year full-time, with the opportunity for renewal, based in the Washington, DC area. ELIGIBILITY: U.S. citizenship is required. An existing security clearance, or eligibility to obtain one, is helpful but not required. DEADLINE: applications are due July 22, 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Connections Through Culture Grants 2026 (British Council)
item.description:
Organisation: British Council

Location: International collaboration grant. Each application must involve at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they apply from throughout the project.

Award: Grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000. Free to apply.

Duration: Project-based; develop and deliver collaborative projects across a range of themes and art forms.

Deadline: 12 Aug 2026

Connections Through Culture (CTC) grants are designed to enable meaningful partnerships, cross-cultural exchange and long-term collaboration between the UK and participating countries. In 2026, CTC incorporates the grants programmes formerly known as Biennials Connect (Visual Arts) and Architecture, Design and Fashion Biennials and Festivals Grants, and serves as the British Council's major Arts grants programme for 2026 (in place of International Collaboration Grants). It offers grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000 to support creative practitioners, cultural professionals, artists, organisations, festivals, biennials, hubs and collectives to develop and deliver collaborative projects across a wide range of themes and art forms, including applied arts, architecture, curating, dance, design, digital, drawing, education, fashion, film, installation, interdisciplinary work, painting, performance, photography, printmaking, public art, research, sculpture, social practice, sound art, textiles, video, visual arts and writing. ELIGIBILITY: applications must involve collaboration between at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they are applying from at the time of application and throughout the project activity period. PARTICIPATING COUNTRIES: Albania, Argentina, Australia, Bangladesh, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Colombia, Mainland China, Egypt, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Kosovo, Lebanon, Malaysia, Mexico, Montenegro, New Zealand, Nigeria, North Macedonia, Pakistan, Palestine, Peru, Philippines, Saudi Arabia, Serbia, South Africa, Tanzania, Thailand, Tunisia, Turkey, United Arab Emirates, UK, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Venezuela, and Vietnam. Eligible applicants include artists and creative practitioners, arts and cultural organisations, festivals and biennials, cultural professionals, creative hubs/networks/collectives, and organisations working across arts, culture and the creative sectors. Note: applications involving projects organised by or taking place as part of Visual Arts, Architecture, Design, Fashion and Craft festivals should be made by the festival or biennial organisation rather than by individual artists, designers or practitioners. No application fee. Deadline 12 August 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Atheras: Nicosia Ludic Lab for Composers, Toyful Music Festival 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Atheras Peoples and Cultures

Location: Nicosia, Cyprus; open to individual artists based in a Creative Europe country

Award: Full financial support: travel, shared accommodation in central Nicosia, and daily allowance. Rehearsal space and full production support provided; final live concert recorded (video and audio) for documentation and international visibility.

Duration: 31-day residency, 23 October to 22 November 2026

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Atheras Peoples and Cultures invites composers and performer-researchers to apply for the Nicosia Ludic Lab, a 31-day international residency dedicated to exploring 'play' as a compositional and performative methodology in contemporary music. Building on the artistic vision of the Toyful Music Festival, the residency creates a focused environment where experimentation, precision, and imagination coexist, redefining how music is conceived, structured, and performed. The residency runs in Nicosia, Cyprus, from 23 October to 22 November 2026, and participants develop new ensemble works that directly shape the artistic programme of the Toyful Music Festival 2026. ELIGIBILITY: applications are open to individual artists (proposals from duets or collectives will not be considered) based in a Creative Europe country, who must demonstrate a strong interest in experimental, ensemble-based practices. SELECTED RESIDENTS RECEIVE: full financial support (travel, accommodation, daily allowance); shared accommodation in central Nicosia; continuous access to a professional ensemble; mentorship sessions and individual meetings with ensemble members; coaching sessions with composer and conductor Andys Skordis; artistic and professional guidance from the Atheras team; rehearsal space and full production support for Phase 3; and video and audio recording of the final live concert for documentation and international visibility. Deadline: 15 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: RAMDAM, un centre d'art: Extended Possibilities Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: RAMDAM, un centre d'art

Location: Sainte-Foy-les-Lyon (near Lyon), France; open to artists legally resident in Culture Moves Europe eligible countries (EU member states, outermost regions, overseas countries and territories, and some non-EU countries); artists from mainland (hexagonal) France cannot apply

Award: EUR 1,350 per resident allowance for subsistence and other costs, plus travel allowance of EUR 400 (under 5,000 km) or EUR 800 (from 5,000 km). Top-ups: green mobility EUR 400 per resident (600 km or more by non-plane transport), visa EUR 120, outermost regions/OCT EUR 175, family EUR 200 per child under 18. On-site accommodation and technical facilities provided. Supported by Culture Moves Europe.

Duration: 45 days, 1 February to 15 March 2027

Deadline: 14 Jul 2026

Extended Possibilities gives artists the opportunity to work on site at RAMDAM, un centre d'art for 45 days, a long period allowing them to benefit from technical support and to present ongoing work to the public. Artists receive on-site accommodation (a collective room for the whole 45 days), access to a well-maintained, up-to-date technical facility, a grant to support their work, and a lump-sum allowance for travel and visas. RAMDAM is an activist space supporting artists in their artistic research; residencies are open to artists or teams engaged in creation, research, or experimentation, with no obligation to produce a finished work: dance, theatre, performance, painting, sculpture, drawing, film, video, literary writing, screenwriting, digital art, and hybrid forms are all welcome. The call focuses on multidisciplinary projects and research that break down barriers between disciplines and challenge their own field of practice, and considerable attention is given to how the work fits into a social and/or political context and resonates with our times. THE CALL SUPPORTS: 1 or 2 artistic teams (RAMDAM can host at most 5 artists); performing artists of all disciplines; projects at early stages of research-creation and/or more advanced (residencies can be writing residencies and/or stage residencies). All artists must be available for the full 45 days from 1 February to 15 March 2027 and must be from eligible countries (EU countries, outermost regions, overseas countries and territories, and some non-EU countries); artists from mainland (hexagonal) France cannot apply. The working and living spaces are wheelchair accessible, though reaching RAMDAM by public transport can be difficult for disabled people; accessibility support is available via Culture Moves Europe for residents with disabilities. ALLOWANCES: EUR 1,350 per resident for subsistence; travel allowance EUR 400 under 5,000 km or EUR 800 from 5,000 km (travel under 600 km must be by green means of transport unless disability or island residence requires otherwise); green mobility top-up EUR 400 per resident for non-plane travel over 600 km; visa top-up EUR 120; outermost regions/OCT top-up EUR 175; family top-up EUR 200 per child under 18 (housing capacity is limited). TO APPLY: applications in English or French; download the residency project and administrative form documents and email the complete application to contact@ramdamcda.org with subject line 'extended possibilities - 2027' (no WeTransfer, no important information in the email body; reduce file sizes; a third document may be added to help understand the work). Confirmation within 7 working days; shortlisted applicants have video calls with the residency committee in mid-August. Deadline: Tuesday 14 July 2026, 11:59 p.m. NOTE: the call is arbitrarily limited to the first 40 applications received (still open as of 1 July 2026), so apply early. Made possible by the European programme Culture Moves Europe.

Open call details

item.title: TED & POSCA Global Artist Residency 2026: Visible Mending
item.description:
Organisation: TED & POSCA (curated by Fine Acts)

Location: Globally distributed; no single location or central studio. Selected artists work within their own communities, with projects unfolding in public space.

Award: Each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 (covering artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation), plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms.

Duration: One-year residency term; community-rooted public interventions developed in 2026 for implementation in 2027. The initiative runs for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

TED (the global platform for ideas and storytelling) and POSCA (a creative brand by uni Brands Corporation) are launching a new kind of artist residency: a globally distributed cohort of artists working across different geographies, cultures, and communities, united by a shared drive to shape public life through art. Fine Acts, the external curatorial partner selected by TED & POSCA, leads the curation, creative direction, and coordination. There is no single location and no central studio: selected artists work within their own communities, following a shared creative constraint (a unifying conceptual framework around repair, hope, and beauty), with projects unfolding in public space, community participation as a key part of the work, and process mattering as much as outcome. 2026 THEME, VISIBLE MENDING: inspired in part by Kintsugi (the Japanese practice of repairing broken ceramics with gold), the theme explores repair as a creative and civic act, inviting artists to consider how wounds, ruptures, and imperfections can become sites of transformation, and to make processes of repair visible through public interventions that spark reflection, dialogue, and collective imagination. In 2026, four artists will be selected to develop community-rooted public interventions to be implemented in 2027. AWARD: each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 to cover artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation, plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms. While the residency term is one year, the initiative will run for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year and building an evolving global archive of civic artistic interventions. Apply by 31 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Wassaic Project 2027 Summer Exhibition Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: The Wassaic Project

Location: Wassaic, New York, United States (in and around historic Wassaic and Maxon Mills).

Award: A modest honorarium for all accepted artists (part of the organization's effort toward W.A.G.E. certification). Artists creating site-specific installations are also eligible for an Exhibitions Fellowship and a no-fee residency of 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027.

Duration: The 2027 Summer Exhibition runs free and open to the public every weekend, 1 May to 12 September 2027. Optional fellowship residency of 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027.

Deadline: 10 Aug 2026

The Wassaic Project is holding its annual Open Call for the 2027 Summer Exhibition, for artists of all mediums, including 2D work, sculpture, video, new media, site-specific installation, performance, text, poems, essays, and publication-specific work. Selected work is showcased alongside a diverse range of pieces and performances in and around historic Wassaic and Maxon Mills; the 2027 Summer Exhibition will be free and open to the public every weekend from 1 May to 12 September 2027. In an effort toward W.A.G.E. certification, all accepted artists receive a modest honorarium to participate. Artists interested in creating a site-specific installation are also eligible for an Exhibitions Fellowship to help realize their work; Fellows are offered a no-fee residency for 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027 (artists interested in making site-specific work should still apply regardless of whether they are interested in or able to be in residence). The Wassaic Project cultivates and supports community for emerging and professional contemporary artists, writers, and other creatives, with a residency program housed in historic landmark buildings that hosts 10 artists each month in the heart of a rural community. APPLICATION: requires application forms, a CV, and an artwork/portfolio; submitted via SlideRoom. Application fee: USD 25 (no participation fee or additional fees). Deadline 10 August 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Sonic Connections Open Call 2027 (Villa Filanda Antonini)
item.description:
Organisation: Villa Filanda Antonini (VFA)

Location: Villorba, Italy (a former silk mill). Addressed to artists and professionals NOT currently residing in Italy.

Award: Fully funded residency covering mentoring, accommodation, travel costs, production costs, and a daily salary, plus a EUR 1,000 residency stipend.

Duration: One-month residency, 1-31 March 2027.

Deadline: 19 Jul 2026

Villa Filanda Antonini (VFA) launches its Spring Open Call to select up to 4 artists and professionals for a one-month residency in March 2027 on the theme 'Sonic Connections', focused on experimental music, sound art, and performances. Hosted at Villa Filanda Antonini, a former silk mill in Villorba, Italy, the project brings together four international artists and professionals to examine the relationship between architecture, landscape, and collective sonic experience. ADDRESSED TO: artists, performers, curators, designers, and professionals not currently residing in Italy. GRANT & BENEFITS: a fully funded residency covering mentoring, accommodation, travel costs, production costs, and a daily salary, plus a EUR 1,000 residency stipend. WHEN: 1-31 March 2027. HOW TO APPLY: complete the application form and attach a CV, portfolio, project proposal, and letter of reference. The VFA residency program has been selected under conditions by Culture Moves Europe, a project funded by the European Union and implemented by the Goethe-Institut. Deadline: 19 July 2026, 23:59 CEST.

Open call details

item.title: Club Nothing: Community Music Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Nothing

Location: Anywhere in the world.

Award: USD 40,000 fund total; four individuals or collectives each receive USD 10,000 to produce a club event, series, or festival. No application fee.

Duration: Event, series, or festival to take place later in 2026; winners announced late August 2026.

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

Club Nothing is a global events series and USD 40,000 fund from the tech brand Nothing, supporting organisers, artists, and collectives creating new music experiences. The fund will award four individuals or collectives USD 10,000 each to throw a club event, series, or festival later this year, backing disruptors bringing new sounds and experiences to their communities through nightlife anywhere in the world. Applications are judged by a panel including DJ and producer Manuka Honey (Marissa Malik), Nothing Global Senior Community Manager and Madruga Festival co-founder Erica Travis, Nothing Global Head of Communications Shavone Charles, Dazed Music Editor Solomon Pace-McCarrick, A&R and music-publishing founder James Supreme, and music-industry professional Layla Mustafa Rock. Applications close 9 August 2026; winners announced late August 2026. Learn more and apply at nothing.tech/club-nothing (details also posted at https://nothing.community/d/59214-want-10000-to-throw-a-party). See full terms at nothing.tech/club-nothing/terms-and-conditions.

Open call details

item.title: Call to Artists: Walden Fire Station and Library (Calgary Arts Development)
item.description:
Organisation: Calgary Arts Development (CADA)

Location: Walden community, Calgary, Alberta, Canada. Open to local and national (Canadian) professional artists/artist teams.

Award: Total project budget of CAD 275,000 (exclusive of all applicable taxes) for one selected artist/artist team. The artist fee for the total project is CAD 68,750 + GST. Budget breakdown can be adapted to the artist's approach, following CARFAC's recommendation that a minimum 20% of the budget go to the artist's fee; the total allocated budget will not be increased.

Duration: Concept and design from September 2026 to end of July 2027; fabrication and installation (Phase 2) from the second half of 2027 into 2028.

Deadline: 28 Jul 2026

Calgary Arts Development (CADA) is seeking an artist or artist team to join The City of Calgary's Walden Fire Station and Library project team to create permanent public artwork(s) for a combined fire station and library planned for the community of Walden. One artist/artist team will be selected with a total project budget of CAD 275,000 (exclusive of all applicable taxes); the opportunity is open to local and national professional artists/artist teams working in various media. ELIGIBILITY: local and national mid-career to late-career professional public artists and artist teams with a minimum of five years' previous experience creating artwork in the public realm; they must have at least three outdoor permanent public art installations, each with a lifespan of over 10 years and a minimum project budget of CAD 150,000 (see the Canada Council for the Arts definition of a professional artist). Walden does not currently have an active community association, so the project requires experience in artist-led community engagement and a strong understanding of alternative outreach. Artists must be available to meet the project timelines from September 2026 to the end of concept and design by July 2027; fabrication and installation take place in Phase 2, starting in the second half of 2027 and continuing into 2028. REWARD: total project budget CAD 275,000 (artist fee CAD 68,750 + GST); CADA is open to adapting the budget breakdown to the artist's approach, following CARFAC's recommendation of a minimum 20% to the artist's fee, though the total budget will not be increased. TIMELINE: applications open 16 June 2026 (9am MT); pre-recorded information session posted early July; application deadline 28 July 2026 (no applications accepted after 1pm MT); shortlisted interviews mid-September; results late September 2026. Eligibility questions: walden@calgaryartsdevelopment.com.

Open call details

item.title: Genesis Mission AI Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Horizon Institute for Public Service (with Renaissance Philanthropy, SeedAI, and Fulcrum Science)

Location: United States. Fellows are forward deployed to US Department of Energy national labs (initial partners: Idaho National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory). Remote or in-person; on-site travel at least once a quarter. Must be eligible to work in the US (no visa sponsorship; US green card holders eligible).

Award: Prorated total annual compensation of USD 200,000.

Duration: 9-12 months, starting fall 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Horizon Institute for Public Service, Renaissance Philanthropy, SeedAI, and Fulcrum Science are partnering to launch a fellowship that embeds technical AI talent across the US Department of Energy national labs to advance the goals of the Genesis Mission, DOE's flagship science and technology initiative launched in late 2025. The Genesis Mission identifies 26 national science and technology challenges critical to US leadership and envisions a national platform connecting supercomputers, experimental facilities, AI systems, and unique datasets, with DOE's 17 national labs playing a key role. Genesis Mission AI Fellows are forward deployed to DOE national labs to bring frontier AI expertise to high-priority scientific challenges. As core members of the technical staff on a Genesis Mission project, fellows work alongside national lab scientists and partners and apply AI to national priorities, from building cutting-edge models of plasma dynamics in pursuit of controlled fusion to advancing state-of-the-art models for foundational materials discovery. Initial national lab partners include Idaho National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, and the Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory; additional partners may be announced later. COMPENSATION/DURATION: 9-12 months starting fall 2026, with a prorated total annual compensation of USD 200,000; fellows are expected to travel to their national lab site at least once a quarter but may work on-site if they prefer. ELIGIBILITY: competitive applicants should have at least 2 years of industry experience building AI models or agents (those with industry experience building AI applications or data infrastructure will also be considered); scientific domain expertise is not required, but any placement will involve some training and study in a specific scientific discipline or energy topic, supported by expert colleagues at the host institution. All fellows must be eligible to work in the United States for the duration of the placement; US employment visas cannot be sponsored, but US permanent residents (green card holders) are eligible. APPLICATION: initial form requires a CV or resume (max 2 pages) and short-answer questions on motivations and experiences (max 200 words); successful applicants complete interviews through partner organizations and national labs, and selected fellows are matched to partner labs by scientific domain and availability. The review uses evidence-based best practices including blind grading of application materials. TIMELINE: application deadline 31 July 2026; final fellow selection August-September 2026; expected start fall 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NEIU Art + Design Studio Art Residency 2026-2027
item.description:
Organisation: Northeastern Illinois University (NEIU), Art + Design Department

Location: Salme Harju Steinberg Fine Arts Center, Northeastern Illinois University, Chicago, Illinois, United States. In-person; semester-long access to NEIU facilities.

Award: USD 800 honorarium, plus a semester of full access to NEIU Art + Design facilities specific to the project (Art + Tech, Ceramics, Drawing, Painting, Photography, Printmaking, Sculpture); supplies can be requested with guidance and approval from Studio Area personnel. Visiting Artists may also exhibit in a group exhibition in the NEIU Fine Arts Center Gallery.

Duration: One semester. Residency Session 1: 25 January - 14 May; Session 2: 30 August - 11 December.

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

The NEIU Art + Design Studio Art Residency strives to create visible connections between Northeastern Illinois University's diverse population of art and design students and working artists who embody these identities, providing insights and inspiration through a dynamic exchange of ideas and techniques. Visiting Artists should demonstrate a commitment to their artistic practice and an interest in engaging with NEIU's students and creative community. Each Visiting Artist is awarded an honorarium and has full access for a semester to the NEIU Art + Design facilities specific to their project; over the residency they showcase their work in a public academic forum, lead a hands-on workshop, and participate in a Portfolio Review Day for NEIU's Art + Design students. FACILITIES (in the Salme Harju Steinberg Fine Arts Center) include a Design Studio with the largest collection of Riso drums at any Chicago university, a Fab Lab (FDM and resin 3D printers, large-format CNC router, vinyl cutters, laser cutters), a Ceramic Studio (throwing wheels, glaze room, electric and gas kilns, raku area), a Letterpress Studio (two Vandercook presses, metal and wood type), a Sculpture Studio (wood shop, clay modeling and spray painting areas), a Photography Studio (analog and digital, plus a lighting studio), and fully equipped Painting, Drawing, and Printmaking studios. BENEFITS: USD 800 honorarium; facility access based on project focus (Art + Tech, Ceramics, Drawing, Painting, Photography, Printmaking, Sculpture), with supplies requestable subject to approval; and the opportunity to exhibit in a group exhibition in the NEIU Fine Arts Center Gallery. PARTICIPATION: lead a 2-hour hands-on workshop for 15-20 students on a topic relevant to the project; give an hour-long artist talk with Q&A; and serve as a guest panelist for a day-long portfolio review. KEY DATES: open call 2 June - 9 August; emailed acceptances in October; Residency Session 1 runs 25 January - 14 May and Session 2 runs 30 August - 11 December. Apply via the JotForm application. Deadline 9 August 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Eamonn McCabe Bursary 2026 (Royal Photographic Society)
item.description:
Organisation: The Royal Photographic Society (RPS), in partnership with Eamonn McCabe's family and The Guardian

Location: International bursary. Applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. Interviews held via Zoom.

Award: GBP 3,000 to deliver a photography project on the theme of 'sporting endeavour'. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS, who will work with the recipient to bring the project to a wider audience.

Duration: The project should be completed within one year of being awarded.

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The Eamonn McCabe Bursary, launched in 2024, commemorates the life of photojournalist Eamonn McCabe (1948-2022), who made his name as a sports photographer before moving to wider editorial work, mainly for The Observer and The Guardian, and who supported a generation of photographers from the 1980s to the 2020s. Run by the RPS in partnership with McCabe's family and The Guardian, this international bursary continues that legacy. AWARD: GBP 3,000 to a photographer aged 30 years or under to deliver a photography project responding to the theme of 'sporting endeavour', which can be interpreted as widely as the applicant wishes, though the general approach should be suitable for publication. The project should normally be new, not previously exhibited or published. As well as professional sports, the call particularly encourages projects covering grassroots and community activities (for example, addressing issues such as mental health or supporting displaced communities), and welcomes creative interpretations of the theme. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. PROCESS: interviews via Zoom in September; recipient announced by the end of September; project completed within one year of award. Apply via the RPS application portal using RPS Account credentials. Closing date 3 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Creative Steps for Individuals 2026 (Arts Council of Wales)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Wales

Location: Wales, United Kingdom. For artists and creatives based in Wales.

Award: GBP 500 to GBP 7,500 (plus access costs). Applicants can apply for up to 90% of the developmental cost; the remaining 10% must come from a non-Arts Council of Wales / non-National Lottery source (in-kind, cash, or both).

Duration: Supports a stage of professional development; allow a minimum of 10 working weeks between the deadline and the project start date.

Deadline: 29 Jul 2026

Creative Steps funds the professional, business and organisational development of artists who have experienced barriers to careers within the arts. Creative Steps for Individuals is open to individual artists and creatives who identify as being Ethnically and Culturally diverse, D/deaf, disabled or neurodivergent, groups that are underrepresented in Arts Council of Wales funding programmes due to barriers to entry. Applicants must have demonstrable experience as an artist or creative professional; those without may still be supported on the basis of artistic potential by submitting a portfolio, CV or examples of work, discussed with a Relationship Manager. WHAT IT SUPPORTS: career development, NOT creating new work or project delivery. Examples include peer-to-peer learning, training courses (not formal/vocational education), building networks, mentorship/coaching, business development/social media/marketing advice, website development/marketing materials/equipment or materials, and setting up a new organisation. Budgets may include the applicant's own time, freelancer costs (paid at standard industry rates), mentor/consultant costs, access costs, and childcare/caregiving costs. AMOUNT: GBP 500 to GBP 7,500 (plus access costs); up to 90% of the developmental cost, with the remaining 10% from a non-Arts Council of Wales / non-National Lottery source (in-kind, cash, or both, confirmed by letters of support). No application fee. APPLY: applicants must express interest at least 3 weeks before the deadline to receive support from an Application Coach or Access Support coordinator; an application form is provided by a Relationship Manager. The fund runs in rounds; the next round (Round 2 2026) has an Expression of Interest cut-off of 8 July 2026 and an application deadline of 29 July 2026. Later rounds: Round 3 (EOI 30 September, deadline 21 October 2026) and Round 4 (EOI 10 February, deadline 3 March 2027).

Open call details

Award: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Project Funding for Individuals, part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds project-based creative activities across all artistic disciplines. Eligible activities include creating new work, research and development, residencies, mentoring, professional development, and time dedicated to specific creative outcomes. item.title: NEU Commission 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Page Not Found

Location: The Netherlands; open to non-EEA-national students enrolled at Dutch art academies.

Award: EUR 2,000 each to three commissioned writers, paid as an advance in September 2026.

Duration: One-time commission; commissioned texts published at a later date

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Page Not Found announces the third edition of the NEU Commission, which intends to support the emergence of alternative voices within contemporary art. It targets a group currently under-represented in Europe: art students with limited financial means who are required to provide a guarantee for their visa and to pay high non-EU tuition fees, economical barriers to their participation in art and knowledge production in The Netherlands. Page Not Found will commission three art students from non-European countries to each write an essay or a free-form text in English. Each selected writer receives EUR 2,000 for the commission, provided as an advance payment in September 2026; the commissioned texts are published at a later date. OPEN CALL: only students enrolled in Dutch art academies who are NOT nationals of the European Economic Area are eligible. The commissioned text does not need to be submitted when answering the open call; the jury selects recipients based on their previous writing and financial need. Review the Rules of the Open Call and the FAQ before applying. Deadline: Friday 31 July 2026, 23:59 CEST (Amsterdam).

Open call details
